validation_outcome: PASS: 0 typecheck errors, 0 lint warnings, 788/788 tests pass (89 blog.service tests, 39 blog.controller tests).
task_summary: ST10 — Map explicit-slug duplicate-key errors to 409. Wrap the explicit-slug create() path and the slug-changing update() path in try/catch using the existing isDuplicateKeyError() helper to throw ConflictException (HTTP 409) instead of letting raw DB errors propagate as 500. Auto-derived slug retry path is unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Explicit-slug create() with a colliding slug returns 409 ConflictException, not 500.
Slug-changing update() with a colliding slug returns 409 ConflictException, not 500.
Auto-derived slug retry path behavior unchanged.
All existing blog tests pass (89 service + 39 controller).
test_location_guidance: apps/api/src/blog/blog.service.test.ts — add new test cases for explicit-slug collision in create() and slug-changing collision in update(). Run with: node_modules/.bin/vitest run --root apps/api from the worktree root.
implementation_context: The fix adds two localized try/catch blocks. In create(), the explicit-slug branch wraps blogPostRepository.save(post) and catches duplicate-key errors via BlogService.isDuplicateKeyError() (checks ER_DUP_ENTRY/errno 1062 for MySQL, 'UNIQUE constraint failed' for SQLite used in unit tests). In update(), the save wraps its catch with the same helper, and only throws ConflictException when input.slug !== undefined (i.e., slug was being changed). The 409 message is: 'A post with this slug already exists.' The auto-derived path (saveWithDerivedSlugRetry) is untouched.
